---
title: "Logging"
description: "How to observe template build logs from the Go SDK."
---

# Logging

Template builds can emit structured logs through `Build(...)` and `BuildInBackground(...)`.

In Go, logging is configured with `BuildOptions.BasicBuildOptions.OnBuildLogs`.

## Go-specific behavior

Compared with the JS/Python docs:

- `OnBuildLogs` is part of `BasicBuildOptions`, which is embedded inside `BuildOptions`
- `DefaultBuildLogger()` does not accept options
- the current default logger always filters out debug messages
- build callbacks receive `*e2b.LogEntry`
- if `OnBuildLogs` is `nil`, the Go SDK stays silent and does not install a default logger for you
- the root `e2b` package re-exports `LogEntryLevel`, but not named `LogLevel*` constants, so compare against typed string values such as `e2b.LogEntryLevel("warn")`

## Default logger

Use `DefaultBuildLogger()` when you want a simple logger that prints info, warn, and error lines.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()

	template := e2b.Template(nil).
		FromPythonImage("3.12").
		RunCmd(`echo "hello"`)

	_, _ = e2b.Build(ctx, template, "my-template", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			OnBuildLogs: e2b.DefaultBuildLogger(),
		},
	})
}
```

Current default behavior:

- debug entries are ignored
- ANSI escape sequences are removed from messages
- trailing newlines are trimmed
- output is printed as `[level] message`

If you need a configurable minimum log level, provide your own `BuildLogger`.

## Custom logger

You can fully control how build logs are handled by assigning your own `BuildLogger`.

Simple logging:

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"log"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	template := e2b.Template(nil).FromBaseImage()

	logger := e2b.BuildLogger(func(entry *e2b.LogEntry) {
		log.Println(entry.String())
	})

	_, _ = e2b.Build(ctx, template, "my-template", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			OnBuildLogs: logger,
		},
	})
}
```

Custom formatting:

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	template := e2b.Template(nil).FromBaseImage()

	logger := e2b.BuildLogger(func(entry *e2b.LogEntry) {
		ts := entry.Timestamp.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00")
		fmt.Printf("[%s] %s: %s\n", ts, strings.ToUpper(string(entry.Level)), entry.Message)
	})

	_, _ = e2b.BuildInBackground(ctx, template, "my-template", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			OnBuildLogs: logger,
		},
	})
}
```

Filter warnings and errors:

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"log"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	template := e2b.Template(nil).FromBaseImage()

	logger := e2b.BuildLogger(func(entry *e2b.LogEntry) {
		switch entry.Level {
		case e2b.LogEntryLevel("error"), e2b.LogEntryLevel("warn"):
			log.Printf("important: %s\n", entry.String())
		}
	})

	_, _ = e2b.Build(ctx, template, "my-template", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			OnBuildLogs: logger,
		},
	})
}
```

## Structured log entries

The callback receives structured `*e2b.LogEntry` values.

```go
package main

import (
	"time"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	entry := &e2b.LogEntry{
		Timestamp: time.Now(),
		Level:     e2b.LogEntryLevel("info"),
		Message:   "Uploaded 'src'",
	}

	_ = entry.Timestamp
	_ = entry.Level
	_ = entry.Message
	_ = entry.String()
}
```

Current build flows can emit:

- SDK lifecycle messages such as `Build started`, `Waiting for logs...`, and `Build finished`
- upload progress messages such as `Uploaded 'src'`
- control-plane build logs returned during status polling

## LogEntryStart and LogEntryEnd

The Go SDK exports `NewLogEntryStart(...)` and `NewLogEntryEnd(...)`, which create debug-level wrapper values for your own logging code:

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	start := e2b.NewLogEntryStart("Build started")
	end := e2b.NewLogEntryEnd("Build finished")

	_ = start.Timestamp
	_ = start.Level
	_ = start.Message
	_ = end.Timestamp
	_ = end.Level
	_ = end.Message
}
```

Current Go template build callbacks do not emit these specialized wrapper types automatically. They emit ordinary `*e2b.LogEntry` values, including debug-level lifecycle messages when `OnBuildLogs` is set.

For the exact exported types and helpers, see the [Go SDK Template Logger reference](/docs/sdk-reference/go-sdk/template-logger).
